version.description: Optimistic lock version – must match current record version

get.description: Retrieve a paginated list of warehouses. Accessible by ADMIN only.

post.description: Create a new warehouse. Accessible by ADMIN only.

get.description: Retrieve a single warehouse by its UUID. Accessible by ADMIN, MANAGER, and STAFF.

patch.description: Update warehouse details. ADMIN can update any warehouse. MANAGER can only update their own warehouse.

delete.description: Soft-delete a warehouse (sets deletedAt). Accessible by ADMIN only.

get.description: Retrieve a paginated, filterable list of users. Accessible by ADMIN and MANAGER.

post.description: Create a user account. ADMIN can create any role. MANAGER can only create STAFF within their own warehouse.

403.description: Insufficient role or MANAGER creating outside own warehouse

get.description: Retrieve a single user by their UUID. Accessible by ADMIN and MANAGER.

patch.description: Update user details. ADMIN can update any user. MANAGER can only update users in their warehouse.

delete.description: Soft-delete a user (sets deletedAt). Only ADMIN can delete users.

get.description: Retrieve the profile of the currently authenticated user.

patch.description: Update own profile. Any authenticated user can update their email, username, or password. Cannot change role or warehouseId.
Ban a user account. ADMIN can ban any user. MANAGER can only ban users in their warehouse.
Restore a banned user account. ADMIN can unban any user. MANAGER can only unban users in their warehouse.

get.description: Retrieve a paginated, filterable list of packages. ADMIN sees all packages. MANAGER and STAFF are scoped to their warehouse.

post.description: Create a new package. ADMIN can create in any warehouse. MANAGER can only create in their own warehouse.

403.description: MANAGER creating in a warehouse they do not manage

get.description: Retrieve a single package by UUID. MANAGER and STAFF are scoped to their warehouse.

403.description: Package belongs to a different warehouse

patch.description: Update package fields (code, price, status, warehouseId). ADMIN can update any package. MANAGER can only update packages in their warehouse. STAFF can only update the status of packages in their warehouse. Uses optimistic locking – the `version` field must match the current record version to prevent concurrent update conflicts.

400.description: Invalid input data or Invalid status transition

403.description: Insufficient role or STAFF/MANAGER trying to edit restricted fields

409.description: Version conflict – record was modified by another request

delete.description: Soft-delete a package (sets deletedAt). Only ADMIN and Manager can delete packages.

get.description: Retrieve a financial report based on packages. ADMIN sees the report across all warehouses (or can filter by warehouseId). When warehouseId is omitted, the response includes byWarehouse breakdown. MANAGER is restricted to seeing the report for their own warehouse.
Retrieve package status breakdown by warehouse scope. ADMIN sees all warehouses (or can filter by warehouseId). MANAGER is restricted to their own warehouse.

post.description: Authenticate with email and password. Returns a JWT access token (expires in 15 minutes) in the response body. A refresh token (expires in 7 days, single-use rotation) is set as an HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite=None cookie named `refreshToken` — it is NEVER exposed in the response body and cannot be read via client-side JavaScript.

200.description: Login successful. A `Set-Cookie` header with the refresh token is also sent.

400.description: Invalid input data (invalid email format, password too short)

post.description: Exchanges the HttpOnly `refreshToken` cookie (sent automatically by the browser, requires `credentials: "include"` on the client) for a new access token. Uses single-use rotation — the server issues a new refresh token cookie and immediately invalidates the previous one. No request body is required; the refresh token is NEVER read from or written to the request/response body.

200.description: Token refreshed successfully. A new `Set-Cookie` header replaces the previous refresh token.

401.description: Missing, invalid, expired, or already-used (revoked) refresh token cookie

post.description: Revokes the current refresh token server-side and clears the `refreshToken` cookie via `Set-Cookie` with an expired date.

204.description: Logged out successfully. The `refreshToken` cookie is cleared.

schema.example: refreshToken=; Path=/api/v1/auth; HttpOnly; Secure; SameSite=None; Max-Age=0
